title: Scottish airport 'being used by Donald Trump to help bomb Iran'

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Prestwick Airport in South Ayrshire is often used by the US military (Image: Colin Mearns) PRESTWICK Airport is being used by Donald Trump to help facilitate the US bombing of Iran, according to a military intelligence expert. It comes after flight data compiled by the Scottish Greens revealed that 24 US military flights landed at Prestwick Airport in the week leading up to Trump’s bombing of Iran , including at least one that came from Israel.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The purpose of the US aircraft which have landed at Prestwick remains unclear – particularly whether they have gone on to take part in the military operation from another location. A Ministry of Defence spokesperson told The National on Saturday that Prestwick "is not being used by the US to launch military strikes" . But Philip Ingram, a former army colonel and intelligence officer, told The Sunday Mail that the Scottish Government -owned facility will “almost certainly” be supporting the war.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: READ MORE: What we learned from this week's Scottish elections polls “The Americans use Prestwick fairly often and it will almost certainly be supporting efforts in Iran,” he said.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Scottish airport is being used by Donald Trump to bomb Iran, says military expert
